response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems experience (low-latency DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and a solid mentoring track record through workshops and courses. However, he clearly misses the core minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ — his C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — and his career is centered on web/product engineering in TypeScript, Node.js, and D rather than cloud compute platforms, distributed infrastructure at platform scale, or formal technical leadership of cloud/DevOps teams.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (microservices at millions of requests/sec)
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing record (workshops, courses, code katas, conference talks)
Broad, adaptable engineering background across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
concerns: Far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience (~6 months professionally)
No production-level cloud compute platform or large-scale cloud architecture ownership
No formal technical-leadership role leading teams or setting cross-team technical strategy
